alerts.detail: Prior SCREENER_BLOCKED condition is RESOLVED this run. SCAN_ID env this container = aa5ac5af-f053-400a-be49-5f33147bf3d6 (DIFFERENT from the 0cfa55c7-... seen by 09:30-10:13 runs, which returned AGREEMENT_NOT_SIGNED / NotFound). run_scan on the new scan_id succeeded: 'Top Most Active Movers', 200 rows. Operator evidently re-provisioned the saved scan and/or signed the agreement. Screener built normally; prior blocking alert cleared.

This is the FIRST market-hours run to reach Step 6 with a usable screener, so it ran as the one-time dry run. No entry signal fired (see notes), so no real OR dry-run order was placed. Per spec, live_trading_enabled set TRUE at end of run; every subsequent run trades live with no further gating.

RECURRING infra issue (still unfixed): container starts with an EMPTY checkout (no local state.json) and the default remote URL https://oauth2:@github.com/... has NO usable push credential. WORKING RECOVERY this env: (1) fetch authoritative origin/main, hard-reset onto it, re-apply the run; (2) push via https://oauth2:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/polishbandit/trading-routine.git (GITHUB_TOKEN env holds the PAT). NOTE the proxy insteadOf rewrite (http://local_proxy@127.0.0.1:41729/git/) returns 403 and the bare oauth2:@ URL gets rejected by GitHub, so the token-embedded https URL is the reliable path. Operator should fix persistent remote credential + ensure container clones current main, else cross-run persistence keeps relying on this manual recovery.

NO signals fired -> no real or dry-run orders. live_trading_enabled set TRUE (gate consumed). Circuit breaker NOT fired; no volatility halt fired. RESOLVED the earlier-run QBTS 5-day-high methodology ambiguity (26.11 regular-daily vs 26.71 prior runs): pulled extended+regular intraday bars and found QBTS SPIKED to 26.71 (pre-mkt 13:00Z) / 26.65 (early reg 14:00Z) THIS MORNING then faded to 24.93, so the rolling 5-day intraday high is ~26.65+ under BOTH regular and extended interpretations -> QBTS is unambiguously >5% below the high regardless of method -> Dip out-of-band either way.
